provenance.source_corpus: Effects of oil sands tailings compounds and harsh weather on mortality rates, growth and detoxification efforts in nestling tree swallows (Tachycineta bicolor)

source_spans.text: In 2003, harsh weather triggered a widespread nestling die-off.
In 2003, harsh weather triggered a widespread nestling die-off.
Mortality rates on the control site reached 48% while they ranged from 59% to 100% on reclaimed wetlands.
Mortality rates on the control site reached 48% while they ranged from 59% to 100% on reclaimed wetlands.
nestlings on reclaimed wetlands weighed less than those on the control site
nestlings on reclaimed wetlands weighed less than those on the control site, and had higher EROD activity.
Cytochrome P450 activity increased proportionally with the degree of wetland contamination
Cytochrome P450 activity increased proportionally with the degree of wetland contamination
In 2003, the date of clutch initiation (in julian days) was negatively associated with most parameters of reproductive success.
